description_long: This guard rail consists of a long, horizontal metal beam supported by a single vertical post at its center. The main rail appears to be made of galvanized steel, with a slightly corrugated or W-beam profile, providing both strength and impact absorption. The surface is metallic and shows signs of weathering, with a silvery-gray finish and some darker, possibly rusted, areas. The ends of the rail are blunt and squared off, lacking any flared or energy-absorbing terminal treatments. There are no visible reflective markings or panels on the rail, making it utilitarian in appearance. The vertical post is robust and likely anchored into the ground using an embedded post method for stability.
description: A metallic, weathered guard rail with a W-beam profile, supported by a central vertical post and blunt ends, lacking reflective markings.
description_view_0: Side view showing the thin profile of the rail and the depth of the vertical post.
description_view_1: Front view displaying the full length of the rail, the central post, and the blunt ends.
description_view_2: Opposite side view, similar to view 0, highlighting the rail's thickness and post depth.
description_view_3: Rear view showing the back of the rail, central post, and the squared ends.
